frames.name: Diplomatic Crisis Frame
frames.description: Presents the dispute as a serious rift in the US-UK 'special relationship' with potential long-term consequences
frames.emphasis: Historical significance of the relationship breakdown and expert analysis of diplomatic implications

frames.name: Trump Criticism Frame
frames.description: Focuses on Trump's personal attacks on Starmer and his frustration with allies not supporting US action
frames.emphasis: Trump's emotional response and repeated public criticism of the UK decision

frames.name: UK Sovereignty Frame
frames.description: Emphasizes Starmer's defense of British national interests and independent decision-making
frames.emphasis: Starmer's justification that he must judge what's in Britain's national interest

frames.name: Military Logistics Frame
frames.description: Highlights the operational impact of the UK's refusal, focusing on extended flight times and base access issues
frames.emphasis: Practical consequences of the decision on US military operations

outlierPerspectives: The Guardian's inclusion of Emily Thornberry's counter-criticism questioning what Churchill would think of Trump
The Guardian's broader European context showing Spain also refused base access and faced trade threats
Fox Politics' emphasis on Trump's personal connection to the UK through his parents' birthplace
missingContext: Legal basis for the initial US-Israeli strikes on Iran that prompted this dispute
Details about what triggered the original US-Israeli military action
Explanation of the Chagos Islands sovereignty status and Diego Garcia lease arrangements
Timeline and specifics of Iran's retaliatory actions that changed the UK's position
Other NATO allies' positions on the strikes beyond Spain's refusal mentioned in The Guardian
